Title: Structural insights into homotrimeric assembly of cellulose synthase CesA7 from Gossypium hirsutum

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase (CesA) family; glycosyltransferase GT-A fold; plant-conserved region (PCR); class-specific region (CSR); 7 transmembrane helices
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cellulose biosynthesis / secondary cell wall biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes UDP-glucose into β-1,4-glucan chains (cellulose) and translocates the nascent glucan through a transmembrane channel
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major structural component of the plant secondary cell wall and cotton fibre; contributes to fibre quality and biomass/feedstock properties
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cotton fibres, especially secondary cell wall of developing fibre
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cellulose synthesis in cotton fibre secondary cell wall; catalytic-site mutations severely reduce cellulose synthase activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhCesA7 forms a homotrimeric cellulose synthase complex resolved by cryo-EM at 3.5 Å. 2. GhCesA7 possesses cellulose synthase activity in vitro using UDP-glucose. 3. Conserved catalytic residues D540, D742 and W784 are required for glucan synthesis, as corresponding mutants showed strongly reduced activity. 4. TM1-TM6 form a glucan-translocating channel, while TM7 and the PCR domain stabilize trimer assembly. 5. GhCesA7 is proposed to function as a building block of the cellulose synthase complex for protofibril/microfibril formation in cotton secondary walls.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cryo-EM structural determination; recombinant expression and purification; in vitro cellulose synthase activity assay with UDP-[3H]-Glc scintillation counting; site-directed mutagenesis (D540N, D742N, W784A) with activity loss; structural comparison with BcsA and PttCesA8
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly contributes to cellulose production in cotton secondary cell wall, especially in 20 d post-anthesis fibre
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly contributes to cellulose production in cotton secondary cell wall, especially in 20 d post-anthesis fibre
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assay conditions included UDP-glucose and Mg2+; fibre developmental stage (secondary wall synthesis, ~20 d post-anthesis) is relevant to interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GhCesA4; GhCesA8; CSI proteins (interaction discussed as possible/known for CesA complexes, but not directly validated here for GhCesA7)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (FPKM expression ratios of GhCesA4:GhCesA7:GhCesA8 approximately 1.0:1.1:0.8); accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper is primarily structural/biochemical rather than transgenic functional analysis. GhCesA7 was studied as a cotton enzyme but recombinant protein was expressed in Expi293 cells for cryo-EM. The paper discusses that secondary-wall CSC in cotton may be heterotrimeric with GhCesA4/GhCesA7/GhCesA8 in vivo, whereas the solved structure is a GhCesA7 homotrimer. Unidentified negatively charged densities coordinated by PCR basic residues and R1000 may stabilize or regulate trimer assembly. Unpublished statement in Discussion notes GhCesA7 mutant cotton fibres are finer and longer than wild type, but this is not core validated evidence in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Provides a structural basis to engineer cellulose synthase complexes for improved cotton fibre quality and biomass properties; catalytic and trimer-interface residues in GhCesA7 are candidate targets for editing or allele design, but in planta effects require validation
